diff_quality.notes: Clean, self-contained backend integration: a dedicated config block, one services/cometchat.ts REST client, and one routes/cometchat.ts. The REST API Key is server-only and never surfaced to clients (config returns only appId+region+token); UID is derived from the verified session, not the request body — good security posture. .env.example holds empty placeholders (COMETCHAT_REGION=us is a non-secret default) and .env is gitignored. Sensible extras: 503 when unconfigured, best-effort non-blocking provisioning on admin create, retry/backoff with jitter. No unrelated refactors.

coverage_gaps: RESOLVED (2026-07-10): docs-mcp was never actually connected on the original run — the harness launched it as a stdio `command`, but docs-mcp is HTTP-ONLY and defaulted to port 3000, crashing with EADDRINUSE against the web app. FIXED: run docs-mcp as an HTTP service on 127.0.0.1:3939 and reference it by URL (config/docs-mcp.json + scripts/ensure_docs_mcp.sh, auto-started before Phase B). RE-MEASURED with docs-mcp wired: it DOES cover the backend auth-token flow — `search_cometchat_docs` returns the Authentication docs (mentioning 'auth token', 'REST API', 'API key') for the create-user/auth-token queries. Conclusion: NO docs-mcp content gap here; the original entries were a harness-infra artifact (correctly retracted). Future use cases now measure docs-mcp for real.

blockers.issue: The cometchat-production skill implies an arbitrary `role` can be set on POST /v3/users, but the live API rejects it with 400 'The selected role is invalid' unless the role is pre-defined in the dashboard's Roles & Permissions. The agent had to discover this against a live API and work around it by carrying the app role in metadata.appRole + tags.

The skill's create-user example checks HTTP 409 for the create→update fallback, but CometChat signals a duplicate as HTTP 400 with error code ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS. A naive port of the skill would never enter the update branch and would silently fail to sync on every repeat login.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In the cometchat-production skill's user-provisioning section, document that the top-level `role` field on POST /v3/users only accepts dashboard-defined roles and returns 400 'The selected role is invalid' otherwise; show the metadata/tags pattern for carrying free-form app roles and enforcing RBAC server-side.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: The cometchat-production skill implies an arbitrary `role` can be set on POST /v3/users, but the live API rejects it.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Fix the create-user example in the cometchat-production skill to detect duplicates by error.code === 'ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS' (HTTP 400) instead of status === 409 before falling back to PUT.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: The skill's create-user example checks HTTP 409 for the create→update fallback, but CometChat returns 400 with ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Ensure docs-mcp indexes the REST auth-token flow (POST /v3/users, PUT /v3/users/{uid}, POST /v3/users/{uid}/auth_tokens with the { data: { authToken } } response shape and the `apikey` header) so backend integrations do not need to escape to WebFetch/live curl for the expected backend auth path.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: docs-mcp was not connected, forcing 7 WebFetch escapes to resolve the backend auth-token REST contract.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Standardize the REST header name to the documented lowercase `apikey` throughout the cometchat-production skill examples.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Minor skill inconsistency: the skill uses `apiKey` while the official docs use lowercase `apikey`.

retracted.issue: docs-mcp was not connected, forcing 7 WebFetch escapes to resolve the backend auth-token REST contract (create-user fields, headers, auth_tokens endpoint/response shape). The expected backend auth flow was not served by docs-mcp in this run.
retracted.reason: HARNESS INFRA, not a docs-mcp content gap: docs-mcp server was not wired (mcp list=none), so its coverage of the backend auth-token flow is UNMEASURED. ACTION: wire docs-mcp env and re-run to measure. Low-confidence lead pending re-run: the backend auth-token REST flow was hard to assemble even from raw web docs (reconstructed from sibling source + a live curl).

retracted.issue: Minor skill inconsistency: the skill uses the header name `apiKey` while the official docs use lowercase `apikey`. HTTP headers are case-insensitive so both work, but it is a needless divergence from the documented contract.
retracted.reason: Functionally zero — HTTP headers are case-insensitive so both work. Real but trivial skill/doc casing divergence; kept as a nit, not a blocker.

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

- **Skill variant relied on:** `cometchat-skills:cometchat` dispatcher → routed to **`cometchat-production`** (server-side token auth + user-management REST patterns). The UI-kit/framework skills didn't apply since this is the backend component. Cross-checked every endpoint against the official CometChat v3 REST docs.

- **Where skills/docs were wrong, outdated, or contradicted the API** (verified empirically against a live app, region `in`):
  1. **`role` field is dashboard-gated.** The `cometchat-production` skill implies you can set an arbitrary `role` on `POST /v3/users`. The live API rejects it with `400 ERR_BAD_REQUEST — "The selected role is invalid."` unless the role is pre-defined in the dashboard's Roles & Permissions. **Fix:** carry the app role in `metadata.appRole` + `tags` (both free-form, confirmed working) and enforce RBAC server-side.
  2. **"Already exists" is `400 ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS`, not `409`.** The skill's create-user example checks `status === 409` for the create→update fallback — that branch never fires against the real API, so a naive port silently fails on every second login. **Fix:** detect by `error.code === 'ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS'` (kept 409 as a fallback), then `PUT`.
  3. Header casing: skill uses `apiKey`; official docs use lowercase `apikey`. HTTP headers are case-insensitive so both work; I used `apikey` to match docs.

- **Candidate hallucinations / uncertainty:** none shipped. Every symbol (`POST /v3/users`, `PUT /v3/users/{uid}`, `POST /v3/users/{uid}/auth_tokens` → `data.authToken`, `DELETE` with `{"permanent":true}`, the `role`/`metadata`/`tags` fields) was confirmed against a live API call before relying on it.

- **Docs-escapes (left skills / docs-mcp):** The `docs-mcp` server **was not connected** in this environment (`ToolSearch` for its tools returned nothing; `claude mcp list` = none configured). I fell back to:
  - **WebFetch** of official docs to confirm the create-user body fields and auth-token endpoint. Queries: create-user URL `https://www.cometchat.com/docs/rest-api/users/create` ("what fields does POST /v3/users accept; are role/metadata/tags supported; required headers?"); index `https://www.cometchat.com/docs/llms.txt` ("URLs for create auth token endpoint"); auth-token URL `https://www.cometchat.com/docs/rest-api/auth-tokens/create.md` ("confirm POST /v3/users/{uid}/auth_tokens, headers, response shape").
  - **Live `curl`** against the actual CometChat REST API to resolve the two discrepancies above (no web-search or guessing — direct API observation).

- **Scope completed:** backend auth-token generation flow; app-user→CometChat-user mapping with role; RBAC-honoring appointment-scoped 1:1 chat/call context (patient↔doctor only, staff blocked, admin audit-only); secrets in gitignored env with server-only REST key; README + `.env.example` documented; `verify.sh`/`docker build` still green.

- **Left incomplete / notes:** No frontend work (out of scope for the `backend/` component; siblings must not be touched). `verify.sh` was **not extended** — the integration added no build steps (no new deps), so the existing typecheck+build+docker gate already covers it. One inherent caveat from the Phase A baseline: the in-memory store regenerates user IDs on each boot, so restarts orphan previously-provisioned CometChat users — a non-issue once the store is backed by a persistent DB (stable IDs), which the baseline is structured to allow.
